annotations.label: Cauchy: real symmetric matrices are diagonalizable

anchor.snippet: every real, symmetric matrix is diagonalizable

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Matrix.IsHermitian.spectral_theorem` unitarily diagonalizes any Hermitian matrix over an RCLike field; real symmetric matrices are the special case 𝕜 = ℝ.

annotations.label: Hermitian condition

anchor.snippet: The Hermitian condition on

annotations.note: `LinearMap.IsSymmetric T` is defined as `∀ x y, ⟪T x, y⟫ = ⟪x, T y⟫`, exactly the Hermitian/symmetric inner-product condition.

annotations.label: Equivalent condition A* = A

anchor.snippet: An equivalent condition is that

annotations.note: This lemma states `IsSelfAdjoint A ↔ IsSymmetric A`, i.e. the adjoint condition A* = A is equivalent to the inner-product Hermitian condition.

annotations.label: Eigenvalues of a Hermitian map are real

anchor.snippet: This condition implies that all eigenvalues

annotations.note: `conj_eigenvalue_eq_self` proves that any eigenvalue μ of a symmetric/self-adjoint operator satisfies conj μ = μ, i.e. it is real.

annotations.label: Eigenvector and eigenvalue

anchor.snippet: Recall that an eigenvector of a linear map A is a non-zero vector

annotations.note: `Module.End.HasEigenvector` (a nonzero vector with `f x = μ • x`) and `Module.End.HasEigenvalue` formalize eigenvectors and eigenvalues.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of algebra (used in proof)

anchor.snippet: By the fundamental theorem of algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the fundamental theorem of algebra as the algebraic closedness of ℂ (`Complex.isAlgClosed`), which guarantees the characteristic polynomial has a complex root.

annotations.label: Spectral decomposition

anchor.snippet: called its spectral decomposition

annotations.note: Mathlib gives the spectral/eigenspace decomposition as an internal orthogonal direct sum over eigenvalues for self-adjoint operators in finite dimension.

annotations.label: Eigenspace

anchor.snippet: be the eigenspace corresponding to an eigenvalue

annotations.note: `Module.End.eigenspace f μ` is defined as the kernel of `f - μ • 1`, the eigenspace for eigenvalue μ.

annotations.label: Spectral decomposition as special case of Schur decomposition

anchor.snippet: the spectral decomposition is a special case of the Schur decomposition

annotations.note: No Schur (unitary triangularization) decomposition appears in Mathlib, so the relationship cannot be stated.

annotations.label: Spectral theorem for Hermitian maps
annotations.note: This theorem gives an orthonormal eigenvector basis in which a finite-dimensional self-adjoint operator acts diagonally, the spectral theorem for Hermitian maps.

annotations.label: Singular value decomposition

anchor.section: Spectral decomposition and the singular value decomposition
anchor.snippet: a special case of the singular value decomposition

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the singular values of a finite-dimensional map and their basic properties but does not prove the full SVD factorization T = U Σ V*.

annotations.label: Normal operator

anchor.snippet: A is said to be normal if

annotations.note: `IsStarNormal x` is the class asserting `star x` commutes with `x`, the normality condition A*A = AA*.

annotations.label: Normal iff unitarily diagonalizable

anchor.snippet: A is normal if and only if it is unitarily diagonalizable

annotations.note: Mathlib has no spectral/diagonalization theorem for normal (as opposed to Hermitian/self-adjoint) operators or matrices.

annotations.label: Spectral theorem for compact self-adjoint operators
annotations.note: Labelled the Spectral Theorem for compact self-adjoint operators, it proves the eigenspaces of such an operator have trivial orthogonal complement.

annotations.label: Multiplication by t with no eigenvectors

anchor.snippet: let A be the operator of multiplication by t

annotations.note: This specific multiplication operator on L²[0,1] and its lack of eigenvectors is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dirac delta as generalized eigenvector

anchor.snippet: is the Dirac delta function , is an eigenvector

annotations.note: The rigged-Hilbert-space notion of the Dirac delta as a generalized eigenvector is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spectral subspace

anchor.snippet: consisting of an almost eigenvector

annotations.note: Spectral subspaces of the projection-valued-measure formulation are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Projection-valued measure

anchor.snippet: is then represented by a projection-valued measure

annotations.note: No projection-valued measure structure exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Projection-valued measure formulation

anchor.snippet: One formulation of the spectral theorem expresses the operator A as an integral

annotations.note: The PVM-integral formulation A = ∫ λ dP is absent since projection-valued measures are not formalized.

annotations.label: Unitary equivalence to a multiplication operator

anchor.snippet: every bounded self-adjoint operator is unitarily equivalent to a multiplication operator

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem realizing bounded self-adjoint operators as multiplication operators.

annotations.label: Multiplication operators generalize diagonal matrices

anchor.snippet: Multiplication operators are a direct generalization of diagonal matrices

annotations.note: There is no multiplication-operator-on-L² construction in this spectral context in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Operator norm equals magnitude of largest eigenvalue

anchor.snippet: the operator norm

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the spectral radius (sup |λ| over the spectrum) of a self-adjoint bounded operator equals its norm; for finite-dimensional self-adjoint operators this specializes to operator norm = max |eigenvalue|.

annotations.label: Spectral theorem for bounded normal operators

anchor.snippet: an analogous spectral theorem for bounded normal operators

annotations.note: No spectral theorem for bounded normal operators is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplication operator version of spectral theorem
annotations.note: The multiplication-operator version of the spectral theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Direct integral Hilbert space

anchor.snippet: We then form the direct integral Hilbert space

annotations.note: Direct integrals of Hilbert spaces are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Direct integral version of spectral theorem
annotations.note: The direct-integral formulation of the spectral theorem is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclic vector

anchor.snippet: is called a cyclic vector for

annotations.note: No notion of cyclic vector for an operator is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclic vector unifies the two formulations

anchor.snippet: there is no distinction between the direct-integral and multiplication-operator formulations

annotations.note: Neither cyclic vectors nor the two spectral formulations are formalized, so their equivalence is not present.

annotations.label: Simple spectrum

anchor.snippet: in the sense of spectral multiplicity theory

annotations.note: The spectral-multiplicity notion of simple spectrum is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decomposition into cyclic invariant subspaces

anchor.snippet: we can decompose the Hilbert space as a direct sum of invariant subspaces

annotations.note: The decomposition of a Hilbert space into cyclic invariant subspaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Functional calculus

anchor.snippet: the idea of defining a functional calculus

annotations.note: `cfc` is Mathlib's continuous functional calculus, applying a function to (e.g. self-adjoint) operators/elements.

annotations.label: Spectral theorem for unbounded self-adjoint operators

anchor.snippet: There is also a spectral theorem for self-adjoint operators that applies in these cases

annotations.note: Mathlib has no spectral theorem for unbounded self-adjoint operators.

annotations.label: Constant-coefficient differential operators

anchor.snippet: every constant-coefficient differential operator is unitarily equivalent to a multiplication operator

annotations.note: The diagonalization of constant-coefficient differential operators via the Fourier transform is not formalized in this form.

annotations.label: Generalized eigenvectors for unbounded operators

anchor.snippet: naturally extends to unbounded self-adjoint operators

annotations.note: Generalized eigenvectors in the rigged-Hilbert-space sense are not formalized (Mathlib's genEigenspace is an unrelated algebraic notion).

annotations.label: Completeness of generalized eigenvectors

anchor.snippet: there always exist a real subset of

annotations.note: The completeness of generalized eigenvectors is not formalized in Mathlib.
